Off Calendar Apr-22-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: RE: DEFENDANT MOSSER COMPANIES, INC.'S Motion To Compel Plaintiff To Answer Defendants Deposition Questions And Request For Monetary Sanctions **Redacted**

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Melody Rissell Leonard, Esq., of RISSELL LAW, APC, (415) 874-7288, for Plaintiff.
Elizabeth A. Stonhaus, Esq., of LITTLER MENDELSON, P.C., (408) 998-4150, for Defendant.

The matter is ARGUED. At the conclusion of argument, the court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant The Mosser Companies, Inc's Motion To Compel Plaintiff To Answer Defendants Deposition Questions And Request For Monetary Sanctions is DENIED.

Defendant brings its motion under Code of Civil procedure section 2025.480. Subdivision (b) of that section provides, "This motion shall be made no later than 60 days after the completion of the record of the deposition, and shall be accompanied by a meet and confer declaration under Section 2016.040." "A meet and confer declaration in support of a motion," according to Section 2016.040, "shall state facts showing a reasonable and good faith attempt at an informal resolution of each issue presented by the motion."

The court finds Defendant has failed to Plaintiff failed to demonstrate she made a reasonable and good faith attempt at an informal resolution of each issue presented by this motion. Defense counsel's "attemp[t] [on the record at the April 2, 2025 deposition] to meet and confer with Plaintiff and his counsel regarding Plaintiff's refusal to produce information and documents relevant to this case" does not evidence a good faith a reasonable and good faith attempt at an informal resolution of the issues regarding the deposition testimony. (See Stonhaus Decl. para. 10.) Nor does defense counsel's response to Plaintiff's counsel's April 8, 2025, late afternoon email demonstrate a reasonable and good faith attempt at an informal resolution. At that point, Defendant's papers were written and, indeed, Defendant filed its papers quite soon after that.

The record does not show Defendant acted with substantial justification with respect to this discovery. Defendant and counsel, jointly and severally, shall pay $500 as sanctions, payable to Plaintiff, payment no later than by April 30, 2025, receipt filed no later than May 6, 2025.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn Clerk: Robert Woods. Court Reporter: Natalie Vaccarezza, CSR: 11415; T: 707.338.2942; E: mdrnlegal@gmail.com (302/JMQ)
